reason: MCQ with paper evidence provided. The incorrect options rely on overclaims not supported by the stem (suppression terminates TR gas generation, eliminates explosion hazard, or guarantees dilution below LEL), and no ambiguity is apparent under the stated conditions.

Short-answer; papers provided. The gold answer is closed-book solvable from the stem using general engineering reasoning about thermal runaway gas generation vs flame suppression, and it is explicitly supported (not contradicted) by the evidence: fire can partially consume liberated flammable gases reducing explosion probability (C006), clean agents extinguish flames but may increase gas accumulation/explosion risk (C007), and thermal runaway produces substantial flammable gas (C001/C004). The required additional design consideration (ventilation/gas management) follows logically to address accumulation in a confined enclosure and is not dependent on paper-only thresholds.
